items.docs: Returns the account associated with the API key.

params.name: status
params.description: Order status to be queried. open, closed or all. Defaults to open.

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of orders in response. Defaults to 50 and max is 500.

params.name: after
params.description: The response will include only ones submitted after this timestamp (exclusive.)

params.name: until
params.description: The response will include only ones submitted until this timestamp (exclusive.)

params.name: direction
params.description: The chronological order of response based on the submission time. asc or desc. Defaults to desc.

params.name: nested
params.description: If true, the result will roll up multi-leg orders under the legs field of primary order.

params.name: symbols
params.description: A comma-separated list of symbols to filter by (ex. “AAPL,TSLA,MSFT”). A currency pair is required for crypto orders (ex. “BTCUSD,BCHUSD,LTCUSD,ETCUSD”).

items.docs: Retrieves a list of orders for the account, filtered by the supplied query parameters.
Places a new order for the given account. An order request may be rejected if the account is not authorized for trading, or if the tradable balance is insufficient to fill the order..

Attempts to cancel all open orders. A response will be provided for each order that is attempted to be cancelled. If an order is no longer cancelable, the server will respond with status 500 and reject the request.

params.name: order_id
params.description: order id

params.name: nested
params.description: If true, the result will roll up multi-leg orders under the legs field of primary order.

items.docs: Retrieves a single order for the given order_id.

params.name: order_id
params.description: order id

items.docs:
Replaces a single order with updated parameters. Each parameter overrides the corresponding attribute of the existing order. The other attributes remain the same as the existing order.

A success return code from a replaced order does NOT guarantee the existing open order has been replaced. If the existing open order is filled before the replacing (new) order reaches the execution venue, the replacing (new) order is rejected, and these events are sent in the trade_updates stream channel. 

While

params.name: order_id
params.description: order id

items.docs: Attempts to cancel an Open Order. If the order is no longer cancelable, the request will be rejected with status 422; otherwise accepted with return status 204.

The positions API provides information about an account’s current open positions. The response will include information such as cost basis, shares traded, and market value, which will be updated live as price information is updated. Once a position is closed, it will no longer be queryable through this API

Retrieves a list of the account’s open positions

params.name: cancel_orders
params.description: If true is specified, cancel all open orders before liquidating all positions.

items.docs: Closes (liquidates) all of the account’s open long and short positions. A response will be provided for each order that is attempted to be cancelled. If an order is no longer cancelable, the server will respond with status 500 and reject the request.

params.name: symbol_or_asset_id
params.description: symbol or assetId

items.docs: Retrieves the account’s open position for the given symbol or assetId.

params.name: symbol_or_asset_id
params.description: symbol or assetId

params.name: qty
params.description: the number of shares to liquidate. Can accept up to 9 decimal points. Cannot work with percentage

params.name: percentage
params.description: percentage of position to liquidate. Must be between 0 and 100. Would only sell fractional if position is originally fractional. Can accept up to 9 decimal points. Cannot work with qty

items.docs: Closes (liquidates) the account’s open position for the given symbol. Works for both long and short positions.

params.name: period
params.description: The duration of the data in <number> + <unit>, such as 1D, where <unit> can be D for day, W for week, M for month and A for year. Defaults to 1M.

params.name: timeframe
params.description: The resolution of time window. 1Min, 5Min, 15Min, 1H, or 1D. If omitted, 1Min for less than 7 days period, 15Min for less than 30 days, or otherwise 1D.

params.name: date_end
params.description: The date the data is returned up to, in “YYYY-MM-DD” format. Defaults to the current market date (rolls over at the market open if extended_hours is false, otherwise at 7am ET)

params.name: extended_hours
params.description: If true, include extended hours in the result. This is effective only for timeframe less than 1D.

items.docs: Returns timeseries data about equity and profit/loss (P/L) of the account in requested timespan.

gets the current account configuration values
Updates and returns the current account configuration values

info.name: Get account activities of one type

params.name: date
params.description: The date for which you want to see activities.

params.name: until
params.description: The response will contain only activities submitted before this date. (Cannot be used with date.)

params.name: after
params.description: The response will contain only activities submitted after this date. (Cannot be used with date.)

params.name: direction
params.description: asc or desc (default desc if unspecified.)

params.name: page_size
params.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response. (See the section on paging above.)

params.name: page_token
params.description: The ID of the end of your current page of results.

params.name: activity_types
params.description: A comma-separated list of the activity types to include in the response. If unspecified, activities of all types will be returned. See ActivityType model for values

items.docs: Returns account activity entries for many types of activities.

info.name: Get account activities of one type

params.name: activity_type
params.description: The activity type you want to view entries for. A list of valid activity types can be found at the bottom of this page.

params.name: date
params.description: The date for which you want to see activities.

params.name: until
params.description: The response will contain only activities submitted before this date. (Cannot be used with date.)

params.name: after
params.description: The response will contain only activities submitted after this date. (Cannot be used with date.)

params.name: direction
params.description: asc or desc (default desc if unspecified.)

params.name: page_size
params.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response. (See the section on paging above.)

params.name: page_token
params.description: The ID of the end of your current page of results.

items.docs: Returns account activity entries for a specific type of activity.

params.name: start
params.description: The first date to retrieve data for (inclusive)

params.name: end
params.description: The last date to retrieve data for (inclusive)

items.docs: Returns the market calendar.

The clock API serves the current market timestamp, whether or not the market is currently open, as well as the times of the next market open and close.

Returns the market clock.
